3.why: Sorting keeps each quadruplet in a canonical order so answers don't duplicate.

4.why: We need the length to bound four nested loops.

5.happening: Start an empty result list res.
5.why: It collects every quadruplet that sums to the target.
5.invariant: res holds all answers found so far.

6.why: The outermost of four nested loops.
6.invariant: i is the first of four indices.

7.why: j is always to the right of i.

8.why: k is always to the right of j.

9.why: l is always to the right of k — every combination is tried exactly once.

10.why: Brute force checks the sum of every distinct quadruplet.

11.why: This quadruplet sums to the target.
This combination doesn't sum to the target.
11.invariant: Move on to the next l.

12.why: It sums exactly to the target.
12.invariant: Adding it to the result set.

13.why: Every one of the n⁴ combinations has been checked.

init.why: Set up the variables the algorithm needs.
